title: Method of preparing a nano- and/or microscale cellulose foam
abstract: The present invention relates to a method for the preparation of a nano- and/or microscale cellulose-based foam. The method comprises the steps of (i) providing a suspension (1) comprising nano- and/or microscale cellulose in an aqueous medium, (ii) simultaneously cooling and agitating the suspension (1) in a mechanical step (2a; 2b) to obtain an at least partially frozen suspension, (iii) freezing the at least partially frozen suspension (5) to obtain a substantially frozen suspension, (iv) treating the suspension under solvent- exchange (7; 8) and (v) removing the solvent (10; 13) to obtain a substantially dry foam (40A) comprising nano- and/or microscale cellulose.
claims: Claims 1. Method for the preparation of a porous nano- and/or microscale cellulose-based foam (40 A) comprising the steps of: a) Providing a suspension (1) comprising nano- and/or microscale cellulose in an aqueous medium; b) Simultaneously cooling and agitating the suspension (1) in a mechanical step (2a; 2b) to obtain an at least partially frozen suspension; c) Freezing the at least partially frozen suspension (5) to obtain a substantially frozen suspension; d) Treating the suspension under solvent-exchange (7; 8); e) Removing the solvent (10; 13) to obtain a substantially dry foam (40 A) comprising nano- and/or microscale cellulose. 2. Method according to claim 1, wherein the suspension (1) comprises 0.3 to 3 wt. %, preferably 0.5 to 3 wt%, more preferably 0.9 to 2.5 wt% and most preferably 1.2 to 1.7 wt% of nano- and/or microscale cellulose. 3. Method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the suspension (1) additionally comprises urea, preferably between 0.5 and 3 wt% and most preferably 2-3 wt% urea. 4. Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least partially frozen suspension in step b) comprises 10 - 90 wt% of aqueous medium in solid state, preferably 20 - 80 wt% and most preferably 30 - 70 wt%. 5. Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least partially frozen suspension obtained in step b) is poured into a mold (3). 6. Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the solvent is a water- miscible solvent with a boiling point below 100 Â°C at standard ambient conditions, preferably ethanol. 7. Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the solvent is recycled (9; 15). 8. Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the method additionally comprises the step of hydrophobizing the foam (40A) comprising nano- and/or microscale cellulose. 9. Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the nano- and/or microscale cellulose is nano- and/or microfibrillated cellulose. 10. Nano- and/or microscale cellulose based foam (40A) obtainable by a method according to one of claims 1 to 9. 1 1. Nano- and/or microscale cellulose based foam (40A) according to claim 10, wherein the foam has a density of 0.01 - 0.3 g/cm 3 . 12. Nano- and/or microscale cellulose based foam (40A) according to claim 10 or 11, wherein the foam has a specific BET surface area between 30 m 2 /g and 100 m 2 /g. 13. Nano- and/or microscale cellulose based foam (40A) according to one of claims 10 to 12, wherein the foam has an oil absorption property of more than 40 L 0ii /kg ceii , preferably more than 60 L 0ii /kg Ceii , and most preferably more than 70 L 0it /kg ce n. 14. Use of a nano- and/or microscale cellulose based foam (40A) produced with a method according to one of claims 1 to 9 for the sorption of fluids, in particular for the absoiption of oil. 15. Use of a nano- and/or microscale cellulose based foam (40A) according to one of claims 10 to 13 as an electrical insulating material.
